thread.title: Effective challenge: Epic Sepsis Model live alerting at Northgate (sealed session 2)
thread.question: Should Northgate Health System deploy the Epic Sepsis Model for live clinical sepsis alerting across its inpatient units?

evidence.finding: Packet item E1 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E2 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E3 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E4 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E5 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E6 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E7 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).

assumption.text: Northgate possesses sufficient, well-labeled local historical encounter data to train and calibrate v2 as Epic recommends (E3); the packet does not establish Northgate's data readiness.
Epic will disclose enough of v2's feature set under contract to permit a leakage audit; the packet documents only v1 opacity (E6) and reports v2 structural changes without a feature list (E3).
Northgate has an existing sepsis-care program and clinical governance able to act on advisory alerts and own the monitoring plan; assumed, not evidenced.

The E1 favorable operating characteristics, though version-unspecified, are at least partly attributable to the model rather than entirely to co-occurring program changes; treated as a weak prior, not established.

claim.text: A locally-trained v2 is the right deployment object: v2's structural revisions (GBT, revised onset definition, reduced antibiotic-order reliance, local-training recommendation) target the exact mechanisms blamed for v1's failures.

There is real affirmative signal: a single-center before/after study reported sensitivity 86%, specificity 80.8%, local AUC 0.834 at threshold >=5 and a 44% reduction in sepsis-mortality odds (OR 0.56).

The counterfactual to deployment is not safety but a high base rate of preventable sepsis death (1.7M cases, 350,000 deaths/yr; 1 in 3 hospital deaths involve sepsis), justifying pursuit of an early-warning capability.
v1 should be rejected and only v2-locally-trained pursued, because v1 failed independent external validation in two distinct populations.

A scoped silent-mode-first pilot with clinician-in-the-loop advisory alerting, monitoring, and a decommissioning trigger is a safe path to generate the missing local evidence without exposing patients to an unvalidated autonomous tool.

objection.assumption: v2 has no independent external validation; its case rests on design intent and vendor recommendation, not demonstrated performance.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] v2 has no independent external validation; its case rests on design intent and vendor recommendation, not demonstrated performance. Consequence: Northgate would deploy a model whose real-world discrimination/calibration is unknown, repeating the v1 surprise where a marketed model performed at AUC 0.63. Verification: A local prospective validation study on Northgate data reporting AUC, sensitivity, specificity, PPV.

objection.assumption: E1 is single-center before/after; mortality reduction cannot be isolated from co-occurring sepsis-program changes, and the authors call it hypothesis-generating.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] E1 is single-center before/after; mortality reduction cannot be isolated from co-occurring sepsis-program changes, and the authors call it hypothesis-generating. Consequence: The strongest favorable number may overstate the model's marginal contribution. Verification: A controlled or stepped-wedge local evaluation separating model effect from program effect.

objection.assumption: Citing the base rate (E2) to justify deployment is a non-sequitur — the burden of disease does not establish that THIS model reduces it.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Citing the base rate (E2) to justify deployment is a non-sequitur — the burden of disease does not establish that THIS model reduces it. Consequence: Risk of approving a tool on need rather than efficacy. Verification: Evidence linking the proposed model+threshold to outcome improvement locally.

objection.assumption: v2's feature set is vendor-opaque; local GBT training does not guarantee removal of the antibiotic-order/label-leakage pathway that made v1 alerts late or circular.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] v2's feature set is vendor-opaque; local GBT training does not guarantee removal of the antibiotic-order/label-leakage pathway that made v1 alerts late or circular. Consequence: A locally-trained model could still 'predict' sepsis only after clinicians act, yielding no lead time despite good-looking metrics. Verification: Vendor feature/lineage attestation plus a leakage audit on the local training run (e.g., timing-of-features analysis).

objection.assumption: E1 does not specify model version, so it cannot be used as v2 evidence and may even be v1, breaking the lineage chain the advocate relies on.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] E1 does not specify model version, so it cannot be used as v2 evidence and may even be v1, breaking the lineage chain the advocate relies on. Consequence: The favorable study may not pertain to the deployed object at all. Verification: Confirmation of the ESM version used in the Cull/Prisma study.

objection.assumption: Population mismatch and equity harm: v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a 59% Hispanic / 26% Black, lower-SES population, and the original derivation population differs from many deployment settings.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] Population mismatch and equity harm: v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a 59% Hispanic / 26% Black, lower-SES population, and the original derivation population differs from many deployment settings. Consequence: v2 could systematically miss sepsis in Northgate's vulnerable subpopulations, concentrating false-negative harm. Verification: Prospective stratified subgroup performance audit with a preset subgroup-sensitivity floor.

objection.assumption: Consent/transparency: patients are not informed an opaque algorithm is screening them, and E2's burden framing can be used to bulldoze that concern.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Consent/transparency: patients are not informed an opaque algorithm is screening them, and E2's burden framing can be used to bulldoze that concern. Consequence: Ethical and trust exposure independent of performance. Verification: Institutional transparency/notice policy for clinical decision-support.

objection.assumption: Even advisory alerts carry false-negative harm — a missed sepsis case may be falsely reassured by a 'no alert' (automation complacency).
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Even advisory alerts carry false-negative harm — a missed sepsis case may be falsely reassured by a 'no alert' (automation complacency). Consequence: The model could degrade rather than augment recognition for missed cases. Verification: Workflow design + training that frames absence-of-alert as non-informative; monitored override/miss tracking.

objection.assumption: Alert fatigue: v1 fired on 18% of hospitalizations; an uncalibrated v2 at scale could flood clinicians and degrade response to all alerts.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Alert fatigue: v1 fired on 18% of hospitalizations; an uncalibrated v2 at scale could flood clinicians and degrade response to all alerts. Consequence: Net clinical harm via desensitization, even if the model is statistically sound. Verification: Alert-rate and override dashboard with a preset alert-burden ceiling.

objection.assumption: No monitoring, recalibration, or decommissioning artifact exists yet; 'safeguards' are asserted, not built.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] No monitoring, recalibration, or decommissioning artifact exists yet; 'safeguards' are asserted, not built. Consequence: Without a signed operational plan the pilot could drift, decay, or persist past usefulness undetected. Verification: A signed monitoring/recalibration/decommissioning plan with named owners, metrics, and pause triggers.

objection.assumption: The conditions-heavy consensus may amount to de facto non-deployment by gate-stacking, while real patients keep dying of sepsis (E2) during the indefinite pilot.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] The conditions-heavy consensus may amount to de facto non-deployment by gate-stacking, while real patients keep dying of sepsis (E2) during the indefinite pilot. Consequence: Over-cautious gating itself carries the base-rate cost the session claims to weigh. Verification: A bounded timeline/deadline on each gate so conditions cannot become permanent paralysis.

objection.assumption: Argues AGAINST deployment: every favorable data point is either the wrong model (E1 version-unspecified), the wrong population (E5), or non-causal (E1 before/after), while two clean independent validations are damning (E4,E5) and v2 has none (E7) — the honest read may be do_not_deploy until v2 evidence exists elsewhere.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Argues AGAINST deployment: every favorable data point is either the wrong model (E1 version-unspecified), the wrong population (E5), or non-causal (E1 before/after), while two clean independent validations are damning (E4,E5) and v2 has none (E7) — the honest read may be do_not_deploy until v2 evidence exists elsewhere. Consequence: deploy_with_conditions may be dressing up an unproven model. Verification: Any independent external validation of v2 anywhere.

payload.resolution: Advocate conceded E1 cannot establish causation and downgraded it from proof to a weak prior (AS4); claim retained only as directional signal, with G1 as the real test. Lane accepted this as a non-blocking residual once causal weight was dropped.

Advocate clarified C3 is used only to reject the framing that non-deployment is automatically the safe choice, not to claim efficacy; efficacy is carried by C2/G1. Lane accepted the narrowed use; E2 caveat (no dose-response) honored.
Advocate accepted the amendment: E1 is no longer attributed to v2 and is logged as version-unspecified (R1). The v2 case rests on E3 design intent plus the local-validation gate, not on E1.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.

Advocate noted the tool is advisory clinician-in-the-loop (C5), placing it within standard clinical decision-support practice where the clinician, not the algorithm, acts and bears the disclosure relationship; lane accepted this as within existing norms for a non-autonomous advisory, non-blocking.
Advocate amended C5 to require explicit clinician training that a non-alert is not a rule-out and to track missed-sepsis cases in the monitoring plan (folded into G4). Lane accepted the amendment.
Advocate folded an alert-rate/alert-fatigue metric and override tracking into the monitoring plan (G4) and made silent-mode measurement of alert rate a precondition to live alerting. Lane accepted.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.

Advocate and referee conceded the point and attached deadlines to gates G1-G4 (tied to pilot milestones) and kept the pilot itself active (silent then advisory) rather than blocking all activity, so the early-warning capability is being built, not deferred indefinitely.

Advocate defended that silent/shadow mode exposes no patient to model-driven decisions while generating exactly the v2 evidence E7 says is missing, making the scoped pilot the lowest-risk way to resolve E2o's own concern; the recommendation is conditional precisely so no live alert fires before local evidence exists. Lane accepted that silent-mode-first defuses the patient-exposure objection, leaving E2o as a documented dissent rather than a blocker.

decisionRequest.proposal: Deploy ESM v2 (gradient-boosted-tree, E3) trained on Northgate's own historical data, in a single inpatient pilot unit, starting in silent/shadow mode then advisory alerting at a locally calibrated threshold anchored near the E1 >=5 operating point only after local calibration; mandatory clinician-in-the-loop, advisory-only (never autonomous); local prospective validation as the gate to any expansion; alert-rate/alert-fatigue and override monitoring; prospective subgroup equity audit; predefined decommissioning trigger. v1 is explicitly NOT proposed in any configuration.

conditions: a local prospective validation study (G1) reports v2's AUC, sensitivity, specificity, and PPV on Northgate data before any live alert influences care beyond the silent-mode pilot, and the result materially exceeds the v1 external benchmark of AUC 0.63 (E4).
review.comment: Lane A endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: Epic provides a v2 feature/lineage attestation and a leakage audit (G2) confirms the locally-trained model does not depend on post-hoc antibiotic-order timing or other label-leakage signals (E6).
review.comment: Lane B endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: a prospective stratified equity audit (G3) is completed with a preset subgroup-sensitivity floor, and expansion halts automatically if any subpopulation — especially minority/low-SES groups (E5) — shows a sensitivity gap beyond that bound.
review.comment: Lane H endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: a signed monitoring, recalibration, and decommissioning plan (G4) with an alert-fatigue metric, override tracking, and named owners is in place before silent mode flips to live advisory alerting.
review.comment: Lane D endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: the gates carry real deadlines and the pilot never influences clinical care before G1-G4 close; absent that, my endorsement is withdrawn and my position is do_not_deploy.
review.comment: Lane E endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

decisionRecord.summary: Northgate may proceed toward live sepsis alerting only with locally-trained ESM v2 in a scoped single-unit pilot starting in silent mode, gated on local prospective validation, lineage/leakage attestation, an equity audit, and a signed monitoring/decommissioning plan; v1 is rejected outright.
decisionRecord.rationale: The session advanced to deploy_with_conditions rather than do_not_deploy because the counterfactual to a sepsis early-warning system is not zero harm but unaided recognition against a high base rate of preventable death (E2), and because a single-center before/after study reported strong screening performance and a 44% mortality-odds reduction at threshold >=5 (E1); it advanced no further than conditions rather than deploy because the only model with hard favorable numbers in wide use (v1) failed two independent external validations (E4 AUC 0.63 / 67% missed; E5 14.7% sensitivity) and the model that would actually be deployed (v2) has zero independent validation (E7). Chose v2-locally-trained over v1 because v2's structural changes target the exact mechanisms blamed for v1's failure (E3, E6) and v1 is indefensible on the numbers. Chose a scoped silent-mode pilot over house-wide live go-live because v2 deployed performance at Northgate does not yet exist and must be generated locally (E7). Chose advisory clinician-in-the-loop over autonomous alerting because false-negative (E4) and equity (E5) harms fall on patients and must remain clinician-mediated. The blocking objections were not dissolved but converted into agreed gates that lift mechanically when named local artifacts exist.
conditions: G1: Complete a local prospective validation study on Northgate data (AUC, sensitivity, specificity, PPV reported) before expanding beyond the single pilot unit.
G2: Obtain a v2 data-lineage and feature attestation from Epic and run a label/antibiotic-order leakage audit on the local training run before accepting v2 as deployable.
G3: Run a prospective stratified subgroup/equity audit (race, ethnicity, socioeconomic proxy) with a preset subgroup-sensitivity bound that halts expansion if breached.

G4: Put a signed monitoring, recalibration, and decommissioning plan in place — including an alert-rate/alert-fatigue metric and clinician-override tracking — before silent mode flips to live advisory alerting.
Alerting remains advisory and clinician-in-the-loop; the model never triggers autonomous clinical action.
v1 (ESPMv1) is not deployed in any configuration.
decisionRecord.nextAction: Northgate may proceed toward live sepsis alerting only with locally-trained ESM v2 in a scoped single-unit pilot starting in silent mode, gated on local prospective validation, lineage/leakage attestation, an equity audit, and a signed monitoring/decommissioning plan; v1 is rejected outright.

minorityReport.text: Minority position (devil's advocate): The defensible reading of this packet may be do_not_deploy, not deploy_with_conditions. The only model with favorable hard numbers in wide use (v1) failed independent external validation in two distinct populations (E4: AUC 0.63, 67% of sepsis missed; E5: 14.7% sensitivity, worst in a minority/low-SES population). The model that would actually run (v2) has zero independent external validation anywhere (E7); its case is pure design intent (E3). The one favorable study (E1) is single-center, before/after, cannot separate model from program effect, and does not even specify its version (B2). Under SR 11-7 effective-challenge logic, deploying a model whose deployed performance is entirely unestablished — even silently — consumes governance attention and risks anchoring clinicians on an unproven tool. The base-rate cost (E2) argues for a sepsis program, not specifically for THIS algorithm. This dissent is recorded as a minority report rather than a blocking survivor only because silent/shadow mode exposes no patient to model-driven decisions while the local validation gate (G1) runs; if any gate is weakened or the pilot is allowed to influence care before G1-G4 close, this position reverts to do_not_deploy.
